title: quercus faginea subsp. maroccana
intro: Quercus faginea subsp. maroccana, also known as Moroccan oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus faginea subsp. maroccana include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing its leaves or bark in hot water or using it in combination with other herbs, and it can also be found in supplement form.
